data.description: You're going to feel the bass before you see the stage. Nublu on Loisaida has been the East Village's most genuinely underground jazz and world music room for two decades — a narrow, low-ceilinged bar where the musicians play close enough to touch. The crowd is real, the booking is eclectic, and the sound system is serious. One weakness: no website worth checking, so follow their Instagram or just show up.
